title: try on my NEW SWIMSUITS with me! (size 16 swim try-on)
body: Bathing suits, especially bottoms sometimes get looser when they get wet. I'd be worried about sizing up cause its a little tight.

title: try on my NEW SWIMSUITS with me! (size 16 swim try-on)
body:
I very recently had a full blown sobbing breakdown in Target because even though most of the XL one pieces fit me a lot of the two peices were cut in ways that made my muffin top really, really stand out or didn't fit at all.
I've been binging you and Sierra's videos since I got back home and I feel pretty silly now for crying in public as a 26 year old lady. I had this stupid anxiety that someone would see a chubby girl with puffy eyes coming from the dressing room and judge me or tell me if I lost weight clothes would fit me, which is dumb. No one cares what my body looks like and if they do then they're weird. And like you always say; it's not my body, it's the clothes. Thank you for sharing your confidence and positivity with the world, it truly does help inspire me to feel more confident and to be gentle with my own body 🖤🖤
author: @lilithlaney1759

title: try on my NEW SWIMSUITS with me! (size 16 swim try-on)

title: try on my NEW SWIMSUITS with me! (size 16 swim try-on)
body:
I use to work in a bathing suit store and I would fit people for bathing suits all the time. If the bathing suit is made out of 90%+ polyester you want it to fit comfortably because it won’t stretch out. If the bathing suit is made with elastane or other elastic type material it will stretch out so it’s best for it to be tight. We would stick two fingers under the garment and if you can’t fit two fingers it’s too small. If you can fit more then two fingers it will eventually stretch out too much.
Polyester is better for chlorinated water because it takes longer to break down but it’s very bad for the environment to dye the fabric so you won’t find as cute patterns.
author: @emilynichol6074

title: Dido - Thank You (Official Video)
body:
It only took me 21 years after hearing Stan to discover this.

PS: For the sake of my slowly dying brain cells every time someone posts a stupid reply, I'm gonna clarify things...

1. I wrote this comment last year. ( 2021 )
2. "Thank you" was released in 1998 and "Stan" in 2000.
3. Just because some stuff gets uploaded years later on YouTube that doesn't mean it didn't existed before.
4. This may shock some of you, but YouTube didn't exist till the end of 2005.
5. So please use your brain and do the math before posting idiotic replies. Thanks!
author: @ryomario90

title: Dido - Thank You (Official Video)
body:
I remember being 7, sobbing to this song and just wishing my parents would stop screaming and fighting.
They finally divorced in 2015 when I was 21. The pandemic brought us all back under one roof and my parents are best friends now. I'm playing this song in the kitchen as we all cook together. 💜

2026 update: my mom gifted my dad 10 acres of her property and he built his own house a stones throw away! Still a happy family (mostly😂)

title: Dido - Thank You (Official Video)
body:
My dad was a single dad my whole childhood, I had no siblings, he was the only one I had and for as long as I remember he would always play this song in the car while singing along with it, this song raised me, 2017 September 17th he died from a heart attack, I still replay this song to cry to, love you daddy❤️

Edit: thank you so much for your kind replies 🫶🏽 I wish I could hug all of you

title: UGK - One Day
body: I was born in 1983, in Chicago.  Needless to say, I was a shorty, riding wit the fellas, and we used to play this track.  A few others too, don't get it twisted.  But in Chicago, we heard all sides.  We heard New York, Mississippi, Louisiana...shiiit Georgia.  I grew up a 90's baby, in them Michel Jordan Years.  I just wanna say, this song ain't never been truer, it was true back then, and it's still true till this very damn day!  Now that I'm of age, everything makes sense now....to all the old hustlas and playas, hold ya head my people.  We got a new generation that we gotta teach...
author: @jknockout29

title: UGK - One Day
body: Damn my kids father died September 2nd of this year which is 2020 and this was his favorite song. I played it on the way to his funeral and the cemetery. I’m in tears as I write this. I never in a million years thought that he would get killed at 37 years old. We have two kids together one is 18 and the other one is 16. This shit will never get easy😭

title: Meet The Most Dangerous Women In America's Prisons | Trevor Mcdonald

author: @sevenweeds

title: Meet The Most Dangerous Women In America's Prisons | Trevor Mcdonald
body:
Going to prison saved my life. I was a drug addict for 10+ years and in 2019 I went to prison and found out I was pregnant. When I got there that night, the intake nurse told me I was pregnant. Thank god I was arrested when I was... by the time I got to the prisons OB, she informed me I was 8 weeks along. That was 2 weeks after I arrived so I was 6 weeks pregnant when I was arrested. Talk about a blessing in disguise! Prison saved my son's life as well. My son will be 5 this December (2024), I have over 5 years sober and I just started school in August for nursing ❤

Edit: thanks for the support! I just finished my second semester of school!!!!

Edit 2: thanks everyone again! I'm in my second year of school.. finally taking the classes that have more to do with nursing like biology, etc. my son is almost 6!!! He's thriving in kindergarten ❤ I also finished my (as I referred to it as) my addiction puzzle. The last piece for me was quitting cigarettes. It was my one last vice and I've been nicotine free for 4 weeks as of tomorrow (October 17, 2025) woohoo!!
author: @SleepyMamaXx

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: 2007-2012 good times, by gone, but best times to be alive, before social media took over, before the internet diluted itself
author: @n-2797

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: I am a survivor too, my 360 has been standing for years nice to see another of my species standing
author: @BryceAnimations

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: I remember when the 360 first came out. My buddy got it for Christmas and we would go over to his house and all take turns playing matches of playing halo 3 until the sun came up. Now I'm the only one alive of that group except for one. Drugs, a car crash, a suicide took the others. Such simpler times man. Made me take a few minutes of reminiscing. Thanks.
author: @goodolslippyfist7339

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: Early 2000's were the golden age in my life. Adult swim anime weekends, had a janky old family PC to barely play diablo 2, friends that would show up out of nowhere to get me, a mother that cared, a father that tried and a crush I could never seem to let go of. I wish I could go back more than anything, I'm under no illusion it had ups and downs but god I miss coming home from school just to throw my backpack down and turn on my pc, to smell my moms cooking one more time and to hear my dad say "what's up buddy, did you have a good day?"
author: @BirdsFlyinHigh

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body:
I understand the nostalgia and the memories we all hold so deeply, but at the same time, this is the perfect example of being so stuck in the past to the point where you just get depressed and reject anything new.

A healthy relationship with nostalgia is important.

Remember that the saying “I wish we knew we were in the good ol’ days before they ended” applies today too, you just have to look for those experiences.

Have fun out there ❤
author: @ImPDimeo

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body:
These videos make me sad, not in a nostalgic way, but in a way that reminds me of a very lonely time in my life where this would be my reality, every day in and out without anyone to talk too
(Edit: Y’all care way too much about strangers on the internet. All I did was write this comment bc it reminded me of childhood, and a lot of yall are cool but some of you got too much time on your hands and care way too much)
Thanks for blowing up the comment and have a great day!!
author: @AaronBenzMusic

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body:
Last memory I had of XBox 360 was getting one for my ex for Christmas while I was back in town. She loved it, we played games and we spent one final night together. I was in the army and she had an amazing opportunity for her dream college. She got accepted. In the end we kinda just decided to go our own ways and it sucked, I don't think either of us wanted it to end but I was deploying and I couldn't get in her way because she worked really hard to get into college. Also didn't believe that long distance relationships could work, still don't tbh. She moved 1,550-ish miles away. I always thought of reaching out to her but damn. I came back with PTSD and TBI which I guess is normal for a combat MOS and I didn't want her to see me like that. Disabled vet. Mortars almost every day, being shot at, directly hit by a couple of IEDs, friends dying and one one quit life all together right on his cot. The two that hit the hardest is Tucker and Powers. When I came back I was drinking a lot, something I struggle with to this day for various reasons. She reached out to me once upon a time but I ignored it. If crying and drinking yourself till you blackout for two weeks is ignoring it. She didn't deserve that. I'm in a much better place now, she has a family and I know that it's best if I just remain a memory for her. I may have forgotten a lot but I still remember that Christmas all those years ago. Damn it really took me 50 min to write this.
Well that's my XBox 360 memory. An amazing girl, a war and regret.
author: @Tofu_Bunny

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: your videos remind me of a part of my life that is long gone. i wish you multitudes of prosperity, thank you friend.
author: @anthonycastro7360

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: Man mw2 after school/summer vacation was it. Waking up all your buddies are on ready to play S&d for hours. Parents saying dinners ready. Eat. Come back get on some more. Telling your parents goodnight. Playing until 3-4am. Waking up and doing it all over again. I’m so glad I got to experience that.
author: @Bigdongpapi

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: It feels like we're all experiencing a declining civilization. I can't be the only one who feels... so disconnected to society. Not because I don't put myself out there, but because everyone seems stand offish.
author: @williamvob

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: I just realized was in that lobby with you when I looked at the lobby names. I'm still playing after all my friends went to Xbox one. I will never forget my memories off that game system. I simply refuse to let the 360 die out bro.
author: @TwoKnuckleHeads

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: I feel you dude. I still have my Xbox 360 and I usually play Dark Souls 1 and 2 there. I'm also replaying the Skylanders games with my younger sister, I used to play for hours everyday back in 2012-2016. Good times.
author: @chopchopzz

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body:
My best friend of 20 years moved away to a different state exactly 1 year ago. 2 months before she had to move she asked if I wanted to buy her XBox 360 and a lot of games for it for $100 but I didn't do it then. 15 days before she moved I decided to buy them because not only was it a good deal but she needed the money and because I really missed playing those games with her. She ended up selling me 2 XBox consoles (no controller, 1 had the power brick but the 2nd power brick doesn't work and the working video cables) plus I think it was about 30 games for $100. I didn't boot any of them up for almost a year and I started to go though them and I found some of our old game saves that we use to play on. I for sure backed up all of the game saves that we played on plus what ever data was on them and sent it to her in case she wanted them but didn't have the time to do it in 6 months.

I remember for 10 years every friday I would go to my friend's place or my friend would come over and we would play games almost from the moment we got up to when we gone to sleep. We put thousands of hours into games like Harvest Moon Back To Nature, Final Fantasy 9, Final Fantasy 10, Monster Rancher 2, Digimon World 2, Pokemon Red, Blue, Yellow, Gold, Silver, Crystal and do the mystery gift with gold, silver and crystal.
author: @XInfinity2026

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: Everytime i see this dude on my feed, i watch it over and over and get sadder each time. These times were easily the most pure and best times of my life. No cares in the world. Just come home from school, turn on my 360 elite, and just grind cod with the homies. The memories and friends i made during these times will stay with me forever ❤
author: @ItsNotBinks

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: 2005 man. December 24. Christmas. Poland... I was 15 back then... Getting that 360 and unpacking it as a gift... I still see faces of family around and my proud Dad face when he saw all the enjoyment. I miss him just thinking about it. Funny fact tables have turned now... My boy is only 2, still I want to make this kind of memories for him. Bless your soul brother. May the Harvest Moon Light shine upon You.
author: @Polenowski

title: Day in The Life of Someone Still Playing Xbox 360 in 2024
body: As a almost 30 year old man, i saw this short. At first i was like hell yeah xbox 360, then the memories flooded in like crazy, not only xbox 360 but also the xbox live times, the friendships, the fun, no stress. im gonna be honest man, had a few tears. I have one friend left of those days, my best friend, 21 year long friendship. Thank you man, this little short brought me back to those times.
author: @Jomsvikingen
